headline: US Tariffs on Canada Escalate; AI Chip Demand Drives Nvidia, Samsung Moves
summary: US-Canada trade tensions intensified with new 50% tariffs on Canadian goods, while the Treasury flagged potentially abusive tax strategies. The AI chip sector saw significant developments, with Nvidia touting new designs and Google exploring Samsung for future TPU production amidst tight TSMC capacity.

bullets.lead: US-Canada trade tensions escalated with new tariffs on Canadian goods.

segments.text: The Trump administration announced new 50% tariffs on Canadian ice hockey sticks and other products, despite ongoing talks to avert such measures.

sources.title: Canada’s Carney Says Trump Agrees to Talks to Avert New 50% Tariff
Trump misses the net with tariff on Canadian ice hockey sticks
Carney looking at 'all options' as Trump announces 50% tariffs on Canada
Trump Escalates Canada Tariffs as Mark Carney Holds Firm

bullets.lead: The coal sector is seeking financial support from the Trump administration.

segments.text: Industry representatives urged the administration to provide financing for both existing and new power plants.

sources.title: Coal sector urges Trump administration to provide financing for existing, new power plants
Coal sector urges Trump administration to provide financing for existing, new power plants

bullets.lead: Nintendo has asserted that consumers are not entitled to tariff refunds.

segments.text: This stance comes amidst broader discussions on the impact of tariffs on imported goods and consumer costs.

sources.title: Nintendo Argues Consumers Are ‘Not Entitled’ To Tariff Refunds

bullets.lead: Nvidia is advancing its next-generation AI chip, Vera Rubin.

segments.text: The Vera Rubin design is reported to offer 10x more throughput per megawatt compared to the Blackwell architecture, with Nvidia touting progress in getting the new design to customers.

sources.title: Nvidia Vera Rubin shown to provide 10x more throughput per megawatt than Blackwell
Anthropic’s Robot Ambition; Nvidia Ramps Up Vera Rubin
Nvidia Touts Progress Getting New Rubin Design to Customers

bullets.lead: Google is considering Samsung for its future AI chip production.

segments.text: Due to tight capacity at TSMC, Google plans to use Samsung's 2nm process for memory I/O dies for its next-generation 10th-gen TPU 'Icefish', while core compute engines would still use TSMC's 1.4nm.

sources.title: Google Plans New ‘Frozen’ AI Chip, Preventing Scams in the AI Era, 160 Software Startups For Sale — TITV [Video]

bullets.lead: SpaceX has made significant orders for Nvidia's AI servers.

segments.text: The company placed initial orders for 13,000 units of Nvidia GB300-based AI servers from Foxconn, estimated at $52 billion, marking its entry into large-scale AI infrastructure.

bullets.lead: UMC is evaluating an expansion of its Taiwan fab.

segments.text: This consideration is driven by an overflow of advanced packaging and specialty AI-related orders, including silicon interposers and deep trench capacitors.

bullets.lead: The US Treasury has flagged certain Wall Street tax strategies.

segments.text: These strategies are considered potentially abusive, leading the Treasury to send warnings to hedge funds.

sources.title: US Treasury flags Wall Street tax strategies as potentially abusive, Bloomberg News reports
US Treasury sends warning to hedge funds over ‘tax alpha’ strategies
Treasury Flags Concern Over ‘Potentially Abusive’ Tax Trades

bullets.lead: Treasury yields saw an increase ahead of the upcoming Federal Reserve meeting.

segments.text: This movement reflects market anticipation and positioning in advance of the Fed's policy discussions.

sources.title: Treasury Yields Higher Ahead of Fed Meeting

bullets.lead: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent issued a warning regarding AI intellectual property.

segments.text: Bessent highlighted concerns over Chinese theft of AI intellectual property, underscoring national security implications.

sources.title: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent Warns of Chinese Theft of AI Intellectual Property

bullets.lead: Dell stock surged following strong guidance from Supermicro.

segments.text: Dell's shares jumped after its rival Supermicro provided robust margin guidance, signaling positive sentiment in the server market.

sources.title: Dell stock jumps on rival Supermicro’s strong margin guidance

bullets.lead: Airbus announced ambitious earnings targets and a share buyback.

segments.text: The aerospace giant aims to nearly double its earnings by 2029 and initiated a 5 billion euro share buyback program.

sources.title: Airbus targets near-doubling of earnings by 2029, launches 5 bln euro buyback

bullets.lead: US chip stocks rebounded, boosting broader market indexes.

segments.text: The recovery in the semiconductor sector lifted overall market sentiment ahead of key tech earnings reports.

sources.title: U.S. Chip Stocks Rebound, Lifting Indexes Ahead of Tech Earnings
Wall St ends higher on chip stocks recovery; earnings in focus

bullets.lead: Meta's AI incubator is developing a new tool to cut coding costs.

segments.text: The incubator is working on an OpenRouter rival, aiming to streamline development and reduce expenses.

sources.title: Meta’s AI Incubator Is Developing an OpenRouter Rival to Cut Coding Costs

bullets.lead: Google released new Gemini Flash models, but its flagship model faces delays.

segments.text: The introduction of the Flash models provides new options, though the delay of the primary Gemini model indicates ongoing development challenges.

sources.title: Google Releases New Gemini Flash Models, But Flagship Still Delayed

bullets.lead: Adobe and Salesforce experienced downgrades amid AI concerns.

segments.text: These downgrades reflect growing investor fears regarding the impact of AI on software and platform companies.

sources.title: Adobe, Salesforce Downgrades Are Latest Show of AI Fears

watchlist.name: SpaceX
watchlist.note: Track the impact of its substantial $52 billion Nvidia GB300 AI server orders on its infrastructure buildout and future AI capabilities.

x_themes.topic: AI-driven DRAM/HBM Shortages and Price Increases
x_themes.summary: Morgan Stanley reports significant memory shortages, with prices up 25% QoQ, and long-term concerns through 2028. AI demand is consuming so much DRAM that it's bottlenecking AI demand itself, as well as PC and smartphone builds.

x_themes.topic: TSMC's Beat-and-Raise on Strong AI Demand and 2nm Ramp
x_themes.summary: TSMC delivered a beat-and-raise quarter, lifting its FY26 USD revenue growth guidance to over 40% and raising CapEx, citing "stronger and stronger" AI demand through 2029-30. A lighter 3Q gross margin guide is attributed to a faster-than-expected 2nm ramp.

x_themes.topic: Nvidia's 9.3% Stake in Nebius AI Cloud
x_themes.summary: Nvidia ($NVDA) disclosed a 9.3% passive stake in Nebius ($NBIS), an AI cloud leader, following an earlier investment. Nebius is evolving into a hyperscaler, leveraging a $775M debt facility collateralized by investment-grade customer contracts to fund capacity expansion.

x_themes.topic: AMD and Microsoft Expand AI Infrastructure Partnership on Azure
x_themes.summary: AMD and Microsoft announced an expanded strategic partnership for AI infrastructure on Azure, with Microsoft deploying AMD's Helios Rackscale Solution, which combines MI455X GPUs, EPYC Venice CPUs, Pensando DPUs, and ROCm software for large-scale AI inference.

x_themes.topic: US Regulations on Chinese AI Models Post-Kimi K3
x_themes.summary: The U.S. is reportedly considering stricter rules on Chinese AI models, such as Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3, including requiring U.S. hosting providers to guarantee security and accept liability, alongside potential procurement bans and export blacklists.

x_themes.topic: Kimi K3's Frontier Pricing and Compute Requirements
x_themes.summary: Despite market jitters, Kimi K3 is a 2.8-trillion-parameter model priced at frontier levels ($3/$15 per million tokens) and runs only at maximum reasoning effort, requiring high compute. This reinforces the thesis that frontier AI capability still demands significant compute.

x_themes.topic: Coherent's Position in AI Optical Networking
x_themes.summary: Coherent ($COHR) is highlighted for its strong position in AI optical networking, driven by 800G/1.6T transceivers, EML/CW lasers, OCS, and CPO. The company's vertical integration and transition to 6-inch Indium Phosphide wafers are key to its margin thesis, supported by a $2B Nvidia investment.

x_themes.topic: UMC's Fab Expansion for AI Advanced Packaging Orders
x_themes.summary: UMC is considering expanding a fab in Taiwan due to an overflow of advanced packaging and specialty AI-related orders, including silicon interposers and deep trench capacitors, from clients like Qualcomm, Intel, and SanDisk, with collaborations on hybrid bonding and memory chip stacking.

Goldman Sachs highlights current market valuations, with the S&P 500 CAPE reaching 41.4 in July 2026, comparable only to 1929 and 1999/2000. The Buffett indicator also stands at an extreme high of over 200%, raising concerns about market sustainability.

x_themes.topic: SpaceX Orders $52B Worth of Nvidia GB300 AI Servers
x_themes.summary: SpaceX has placed its first orders for Nvidia GB300-based AI servers with Foxconn, with plans to acquire 13,000 units totaling an estimated $52 billion. This marks SpaceX's entry into large-scale AI server procurement from Foxconn.

x_themes.topic: Kimi K3's KV Cache and Memory Tiering for AI
x_themes.summary: Discussions around Kimi K3's smaller KV cache highlight the continued importance of memory tiering across HBM, DRAM, and NAND for large-scale AI inference systems. This architecture optimizes GPU utilization and manages the vast capacity and cost gaps between different memory types.

x_themes.topic: IREN's $4B+ ARR Target from AI Cloud Contracts
x_themes.summary: IREN surged after revising its ARR target to over $4 billion, driven by new AI cloud contracts with major customers including Microsoft, Nvidia, Perplexity, and Figure. This indicates strong growth for AI cloud and colocation providers.

x_themes.topic: Taiwan Proposal for On-Site Power for Fabs and AI Data Centers
x_themes.summary: Taiwan lawmakers are reviewing a proposal to require major electricity consumers, including TSMC and AI data centers, to install their own power supply for contracts of 5MW or more. This aims to alleviate strain on Taiwan's power grid amidst rising energy demands from the semiconductor and AI sectors.

x_themes.topic: US Oil Refiners Vulnerable to Breakdowns Amid Max Capacity
x_themes.summary: US oil refiners are operating at or above 95% utilization, making them highly vulnerable to equipment breakdowns, especially during hurricane season. This tight market, exacerbated by global conflicts, could lead to significant fuel price volatility.
